name: Vía de la Estrella and La Quebrada Lake Loop
name_original: Villa del Rey y La Quebrada desde Brozas — circular por la Vía de la Estrella
description: Hike the challenging 12.0-mile (19.3 km) Villa del Rey y La Quebrada circular route through dehesa landscapes and historical Vía de la Estre
region_intro: Hiking around Brozas offers exploration through diverse landscapes in the Cáceres province of Spain. The region is characterized by its dehesa landscapes, an agroforestry system featuring open woodland pastures with scattered oak trees. Situated within the Tagus International Biosphere Reserve, the area encompasses valuable ecosystems and prominent natural features like the Tagus River, La Quebrada Lake, and the Alcántara Reservoir. These water bodies and the surrounding dehesa provide varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Brozas The most popular hiking route is Brozas — circular through the city and La Quebrada, an easy 3.6 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This route offers a gentle exploration of the town and its nearby lake. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alto Peña Carnicera desde Mata de Alcántara — circular, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This trail provides views of the surrounding dehesa and takes approximately 2 hours 22 minutes. Local hikers also love the Vía de la Estrella and La Quebrada Lake Loop, a difficult 12.0 miles (19.3 km) trail leading through dehesa landscapes and past La Quebrada Lake, often completed in about 5 hours. Hiking around Brozas is defined by dehesa landscapes, the Tagus River, and reservoirs, offering a mix of ecological and historical interest. The network provides options for easy walks, moderate excursions, and more challenging long-distance paths. The routes in Brozas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Brozas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there around Brozas?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails around Brozas, offering a variety of experiences through the region's unique dehesa landscapes and along water bodies like La Quebrada Lake and the Alcántara Reservoir. These routes range from easy strolls to more challenging long-distance paths.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Brozas?
A: Hikers in Brozas will primarily explore the characteristic &lt;strong&gt;dehesa landscapes&lt;/strong&gt;, which are open woodland pastures with scattered oak trees. The region is also part of the Tagus International Biosphere Reserve, so you'll encounter diverse ecosystems, including areas around the Tagus River, La Quebrada Lake, and the Alcántara Reservoir.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Brozas?
A: Yes, Brozas offers several easy routes suitable for families. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294387935/brozas-city-tour-circular-through-the-city" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brozas City Tour — circular through the city&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 2 km and takes about 30 minutes, offering a gentle exploration of the town's historical areas.

Q: What are some challenging hiking options in the Brozas area?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304795530/via-de-la-estrella-and-la-quebrada-lake-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Estrella and La Quebrada Lake Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 19.3 km trail. This route leads through extensive dehesa landscapes and past La Quebrada Lake, typically requiring around 5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Brozas?
A: Yes, many routes in Brozas are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294405862/brozas-circular-through-the-city-and-la-quebrada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brozas — circular through the city and La Quebrada&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.7 km loop that explores the town and its nearby lake.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along the trails?
A: The region is rich in both natural and historical sights. You can explore the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brozas&lt;/a&gt; itself, including its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5582877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square&lt;/a&gt;. Further afield, the impressive Alcántara Roman Bridge is a notable historical landmark, and the Tagus River, La Quebrada Lake, and Alcántara Reservoir offer beautiful natural settings.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Brozas?
A: The best time to hike in Brozas is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making longer hikes less comfortable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting on the trails?
A: Absolutely. Being part of the Tagus International Biosphere Reserve, the dehesa landscapes around Brozas are home to diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for various bird species, including those associated with the Tagus River, and other native animals that thrive in this unique ecosystem.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Brozas?
A: The hiking routes in Brozas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique dehesa landscapes, the tranquility of the natural areas, and the blend of historical and natural attractions found along the trails.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, routes like the 'Balcón del Mundo Route' (not in this guide, but in the region) are known for impressive viewpoints, including those overlooking the Alcántara Roman Bridge. Many trails traversing the dehesa and along the reservoirs also provide expansive scenic vistas of the surrounding countryside.

Q: Can I find moderate difficulty hikes in the Brozas area?
A: Yes, there are several moderate hikes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294295966/alto-pena-carnicera-desde-mata-de-alcantara-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Peña Carnicera desde Mata de Alcántara — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.7 km path that offers views of the dehesa and takes approximately 2 hours 22 minutes to complete.
tips: The Plaza Mayor of Brozas (Cáceres, Extremadura) is the historic heart of this town, known for its rich heritage and traditional atmosphere. Although not as monumental as those of Cáceres or Trujillo, it has a special charm due to its history and vernacular architecture.

Brozas Castle, also known as “Castillo de la Comienda Mayor de la Orden de Alcántara” and popularly called “the Palace”, is a fortification located in the Spanish town of Brozas, in the province of Cáceres. Its history dates back to the 14th century, although its current appearance comes from a reconstruction carried out in the 16th-17th centuries.

Great stretch of hiking on the pilgrimage route that joins the historic city of Cáceres from the Vía de la Plata path that crosses the Tagus International Biosphere Reserve and connects with the Portuguese Way.

Villa del Rey is a Spanish municipality located in the province of Cáceres, in the autonomous community of Extremadura. Its orography is characterized by wide plains with gentle slopes, and the entire municipality is contained by the hydrographic basin of the Tagus River, with the Jartín River being its main aquifer.
Sanctuary for bird lovers. The waters of the lake and the surrounding natural landscape of La Quebrada create the perfect outdoor gift for nature lovers.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Villa del Rey y La Quebrada circular hike?
faqs.answer: This challenging circular hike is estimated to take approximately 5 hours to complete, covering a distance of 19.3 kilometers.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as a "difficult" hike and requires a very good level of fitness. While paths are generally accessible, sure-footedness is needed due to potentially uneven or rocky sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Brozas. You can typically find parking near the Main Square of Brozas, which is a good starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: What historical sites or natural features will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich blend of history and nature. You'll pass through the charming Main Square of Brozas and by the historic Castillo de Brozas. A significant portion follows The Vía de la Estrella, an ancient Roman and pilgrimage route. You'll also encounter the Church of Santiago of Villa del Rey and the serene La Quebrada Lake, all while traversing characteristic "dehesa" landscapes with oak and cork trees.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Villa del Rey y La Quebrada trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of "dehesa" landscapes, which are open woodlands with oak and cork trees, often used for grazing livestock. You can expect to share the landscape with herds of cows. While generally accessible, some sections may be uneven or rocky, requiring sure-footedness.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on this trail. However, as the route traverses "dehesa" landscapes where livestock graze, it's highly recommended to keep your dog on a leash to ensure the safety of both your pet and the local animals.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, making the 5-hour hike more enjoyable. Summers can be very hot in Extremadura, and winters can be damp.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino de Villa del Rey a Navas del Madroño.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, it's essential to bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months. Sturdy hiking boots are recommended for the uneven terrain. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is advisable due to the open "dehesa" landscapes. Snacks, a map or GPS device, and a first-aid kit are also good to have.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or pubs near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Brozas, where you can find local amenities such as cafes and pubs to refuel before or after your hike. Villa del Rey also offers some basic services.

waypoints.name: The Vía de la Estrella: Brozas to Villa del Rey
